title: test_plan_authored event payload
$comment: Provenance: authored 2026-04-23 in WU 3.2 as the first QA-emitted per-type schema. Fields: plan_path (required, string — canonical /product/test-plans/ path) + test_count (required, integer ≥1). Follows the minimalist precedent of template_coverage_checked (WU 2.6): downstream consumers can reason about the event without re-reading the plan file, but the canonical plan content lives in the file itself.
description: Payload shape for the `test_plan_authored` event emitted by the QA agent after a successful qa-authoring run. The event is the signal that a plan exists at `plan_path` and carries `test_count` tests; qa-execution, qa-regression, and qa-curation all key off subsequent events (`qa_execution_completed`, `qa_execution_failed`, `qa_regression_filed`, `qa_regression_resolved`, `regression_suite_curated`) rather than this one, but a feature missing `test_plan_authored` has not completed its qa-authoring gate.

plan_path.description: Path to the authored test plan file, relative to the product specs repo root. Architecture §4.3 fixes `/product/test-plans/` as the canonical location; a path outside this directory is a mis-emission.

test_count.description: Number of entries in the plan's `tests` array at emission time. Duplicated into the payload so a consumer reading the event log without pulling the plan file can still reason about coverage breadth.
